In transaction mode, partitions with invisible sub-transaction data must not be pruned
Empty partitions must contribute zero rows to query results
Partition selection must preserve query semantics (no data loss)
performance_impact: Beneficial when tables have many empty partitions, reducing I/O and scan overhead. May add metadata lookup cost. Most impactful on large partitioned tables with sparse data distribution. Minimal benefit on small tables or when most partitions contain data.
edge_cases: Tables where all partitions are empty (becomes LogicalEmptyRelation)
Transaction load scenarios with invisible sub-transaction data
Dynamic partition creation during query execution
Stale partition metadata causing incorrect pruning
Queries requiring exact partition count for debugging/monitoring
framework_notes: Matches LogicalOlapScan group expressions in Cascades memo. Returns null if no partitions pruned (no transformation). Returns LogicalEmptyRelation if all partitions empty. Does not trigger enforcer insertion as it's a logical equivalence transformation. Operates during logical optimization phase before physical planning.

summary: This rule matches Logical CTE Consumer operators to collect metadata about which producer output slots are referenced, storing this in the statement context to facilitate CTE column pruning.
ra_condition: Operator type is LogicalCTEConsumer
correctness_conditions: Must execute prior to CTE column pruning transformations
CTE Producer definition must exist for the given CteId
ProducerToConsumerOutputMap must be accurately derived from query semantics
performance_impact: Enables CTE column pruning which reduces memory and network overhead; optimization time impact is minimal.
edge_cases: Recursive CTEs may have complex output dependencies
Multiple consumers for the same CTE aggregate requirements correctly
CTEs without consumers do not trigger this rule
framework_notes: Matches LogicalCTEConsumer group expressions; updates StatementContext side-effectively without changing Memo structure; does not trigger physical enforcers.

summary: Transforms LogicalApply nodes representing scalar subqueries into LogicalJoin nodes, handling both correlated and uncorrelated cases to enable standard join optimizations.
ra_condition: S returns \le 1 row, correlation predicates are equality
correctness_conditions: The right child (subquery) must return at most one row
Correlation predicates must be equality operators (EqualTo)
Correlation filter must be present if correlated slots exist
performance_impact: Beneficial by converting Apply (nested loop) to Join (hash/merge), enabling reordering and pushdown; potential runtime overhead if AssertNumRows fails for uncorrelated subqueries.
edge_cases: Non-equality correlation predicates throw AnalysisException
Uncorrelated subqueries returning >1 row fail at runtime via AssertNumRows
Correlation filter eliminated by SimplifyConflictCompound rule falls back to uncorrelated logic
framework_notes: Matches LogicalApply group expressions in Memo; transforms to LogicalJoin without direct Enforcer insertion; relies on downstream rules for distribution planning.

summary: Validates constraints on multiple DISTINCT aggregate functions within a LogicalAggregate, ensuring engine support for multi-distinct scenarios and preventing unsupported multi-column distinct combinations.
ra_condition: |DistinctArgs(F)| > 1
correctness_conditions: All distinct functions must implement SupportMultiDistinct interface if multiple distinct arguments exist
Multi-column distinct functions (arity > 1) cannot coexist with other distinct functions
OrderExpression arguments in distinct functions are exempt from multi-column checks
performance_impact: No runtime performance impact; acts as a compile-time validation to prevent unsupported query execution plans that would fail or behave incorrectly.
edge_cases: Queries with COUNT(DISTINCT col1, col2) combined with SUM(DISTINCT col3)
Aggregate functions not implementing SupportMultiDistinct used in multi-distinct context
GROUP_CONCAT with distinct and order by clauses
framework_notes: Registered as RuleType.CHECK_ANALYSIS, executing during analysis phase; matches LogicalAggregate group expressions; throws AnalysisException on violation instead of transforming the plan; does not trigger Enforcer insertion.

summary: This rule infers implicit IS NOT NULL predicates from existing filter conditions (e.g., col = 5 implies col IS NOT NULL) and explicitly adds them to the filter conjuncts to assist downstream optimizations.
ra_condition: q is the non-empty set of IS NOT NULL predicates logically implied by p, and q is not already a subset of p
correctness_conditions: The inference logic (ExpressionUtils.inferNotNull) must be sound (p logically implies q)
Adding q must not change the result set (semantic equivalence holds)
The rule must terminate (prevented by checking for generated flags in the 'when' clause)
performance_impact: Beneficial for downstream operators (joins, aggregations) that optimize based on NOT NULL constraints. Evaluation cost of IS NOT NULL is negligible, but adds minor optimization overhead.
edge_cases: Predicates that do not imply non-nullity (e.g., col IS NULL)
Columns already defined as NOT NULL in schema (redundant but harmless)
Filters already containing generated IS NOT NULL predicates (rule skips via 'when' clause)
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter GroupExpressions in the Memo. Uses a 'generated' flag on Not expressions to prevent infinite recursion. Operates within the Memo without triggering physical Enforcers.

summary: This rule normalizes logical plans by ensuring all predicate expressions (conjuncts) in Filter and Join operators explicitly return the Boolean type, applying casts where necessary.
ra_condition: ∃ expr ∈ p such that type(expr) != BooleanType
correctness_conditions: Type coercion must preserve semantic truth value (e.g., non-zero to true)
Null handling in coercion must match SQL boolean semantics
Target type must be strictly BooleanType.INSTANCE
performance_impact: Generally neutral; prevents potential runtime type inference overhead and ensures consistency for downstream physical rules.
edge_cases: Expressions with types incompatible with Boolean casting
Complex nested expressions where type inference is ambiguous
Queries relying on implicit non-boolean truthiness not supported by cast utils
framework_notes: Visits LogicalFilter and LogicalJoin nodes; modifies GroupExpression in Memo if changed; does not trigger Physical Enforcer insertion as it is a logical normalization.

summary: Eliminates redundant pairs of Encode and Decode functions within Logical Project expressions when compression materialization is enabled, simplifying the expression tree.
ra_condition: f and g are inverse Encode/Decode functions AND session.enableCompressMaterialize = true
correctness_conditions: Encode and Decode operations are lossless inverses
Null handling semantics are preserved
Result data type remains compatible with downstream operators
performance_impact: Improves performance by removing unnecessary CPU-intensive serialization and deserialization steps.
edge_cases: Inactive when enableCompressMaterialize session variable is false
Only processes expressions wrapped in Alias nodes
Fails to match if Encode/Decode are not direct children (e.g. separated by Cast)
framework_notes: Matches LogicalProject group expressions. Uses ConnectContext for session variable checks. Note: The provided snippet has a boolean logic error in 'hasNewProjections' that may prevent the rewrite from being applied when changes occur.

summary: This rule pushes filter predicates down through a Join operator to its left or right children, or converts eligible equality predicates into join conditions, based on the join type and column references to optimize query execution.
ra_condition: Predicates reference columns from only one side, and JoinType allows pushdown (e.g., INNER_JOIN allows both, LEFT_OUTER_JOIN allows left)
correctness_conditions: Predicates pushed to the null-supplying side of an outer join must not filter out null-extended rows (enforced by COULD_PUSH_THROUGH lists)
Equality predicates referencing both sides can be converted to join conditions only for INNER_JOIN
Predicates containing unique functions (UDFs) are not pushed down to preserve evaluation order and semantics
Constant predicates (no slots) are pushed to both sides as they apply globally
performance_impact: Significantly reduces the number of rows processed by the Join operator and subsequent network shuffles, improving latency and resource usage. Generally beneficial, though aggressive pushdown on outer joins must respect null semantics.
edge_cases: Outer joins where predicates on the null-supplying side would change semantics (effectively turning outer join into inner join)
Predicates involving User Defined Functions (UDFs) which are kept above the join
Cross joins where all single-side predicates can be pushed down
Join conditions extracted from filters only for Inner Joins via convertJoinCondition
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter on LogicalJoin group expressions. It is a Logical RBO rule, so it does not trigger physical Enforcer insertion. It transforms the plan tree within the Memo structure using PlanUtils.filterOrSelf to avoid empty Filter nodes, potentially enabling further optimizations on child groups.

summary: Eliminates LogicalAggregate operators when grouping columns are already unique and not null in the child plan, rewriting aggregate functions to simple expressions or constants.
ra_condition: Columns G are unique and NOT NULL in E
correctness_conditions: Grouping columns must form a unique key in the input relation
Grouping columns must not contain NULL values
Aggregate functions must be supported (e.g., Sum, Count, Min, Max) and rewritten to preserve null semantics
Output data types must be preserved via casting
performance_impact: Significantly reduces cost by removing hashing or sorting required for aggregation. Beneficial when scanning unique keys or pre-aggregated data. No negative performance impact if conditions are met.
edge_cases: Queries with unsupported aggregate functions (e.g., custom UDAFs) will not trigger the rule
Requires statistical traits (uniqueness) to be propagated from leaf nodes
Does not apply if grouping columns allow NULLs
Complex aggregate functions like Grouping Sets are not supported
framework_notes: Matches LogicalAggregate pattern in Cascades. Checks LogicalProperties traits for uniqueness. Rewrites to Project operator. RBO rule applied during optimization phase.

summary: Pushes filter predicates down below a Window operator if the predicates only reference columns used as partition keys in the Window function, reducing the input size for window calculation.
ra_condition: P_{lower} references only columns in K, where K is the set of common partition keys across all window expressions
correctness_conditions: Filter predicates must depend exclusively on columns defined in the Window partition key list
All window expressions within the operator must share the same partition keys to ensure consistent partitioning
Removing rows based on partition keys eliminates entire partitions, which does not affect window calculations within remaining partitions
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing the number of rows processed by the Window operator, which involves sorting and buffering. No negative impact expected as semantics are preserved for partition-key filters.
edge_cases: Predicates referencing columns used in ORDER BY clause of Window but not PARTITION BY
Predicates involving expressions mixing partition keys with other columns (e.g., c2 + c3 = 1)
Multiple window functions with different partition key definitions
Queries where no filter predicates reference partition keys
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter child of LogicalWindow in the Memo. Returns null if no predicates can be pushed (bottomConjuncts empty). Operates on LogicalPlan nodes before physical enforcement.

summary: This rule pushes down filter predicates that compare a MAX or MIN aggregate result to a literal constant into the input of the aggregate operator, transforming the predicate to compare the underlying column directly to reduce data volume before aggregation.
ra_condition: agg is MAX or MIN, op is > or >= for MAX ( < or <= for MIN), group by expressions are not empty, exactly one aggregate function
correctness_conditions: The aggregate operator must contain exactly one aggregate function which is either MAX or MIN
The filter predicate must compare the aggregate output slot to a literal constant
The comparison operator must be directionally compatible (MAX with >/>=, MIN with </<=)
The aggregate must have non-empty Group By expressions (per implementation constraint)
Predicate normalization must ensure literal is on the right side of the comparison
performance_impact: Beneficial when the pushed predicate is selective, significantly reducing the number of rows processed by the aggregation operator. Minimal overhead as the original filter conjunct is removed from the upper plan.
edge_cases: Aggregates with multiple aggregate functions are ignored
Global aggregates (no Group By) are ignored by this specific rule implementation
Incompatible operators (e.g., MAX < literal) are not transformed to preserve semantics
Predicates with non-literal RHS or complex expressions are not matched
Depends on predicate normalization (literal on right) to match
framework_notes: Implemented as an RBO rule in Doris Cascades framework. Matches LogicalFilter over LogicalAggregate in the Memo. Uses Expression ID matching to correlate filter slots with aggregate outputs. Does not trigger physical property enforcers directly.

summary: Transforms a Logical Aggregate followed by a Left Semi/Anti Join into a Join followed by an Aggregate, pushing the Join below the Aggregate when join conditions reference only grouping columns.
ra_condition: Join is Left Semi/Anti, θ columns ⊆ G, Aggregate is not scalar
correctness_conditions: Join type must be Left Semi or Left Anti (preserves left row cardinality semantics)
Join condition columns must be a subset of Aggregate grouping columns
Aggregate must not be a scalar aggregation (must have grouping keys)
Join must not be a Mark Join
Session variable disable_join_reorder must be false
performance_impact: Beneficial when the Semi/Anti Join is highly selective, reducing rows before expensive aggregation, or to enable join reordering below the aggregation barrier. Potentially harmful if the Aggregate significantly reduces cardinality and the Join is not selective, increasing join processing cost.
edge_cases: Scalar aggregations (empty grouping set)
Join conditions referencing non-grouping columns
Mark Joins used for subquery decorrelation
Queries where session variable disables join reordering
framework_notes: Matches LogicalJoin with LogicalAggregate as left child in the Memo. As a logical equivalence rule, it does not trigger physical Enforcer insertion but transforms GroupExpressions to enable further join reorder optimizations below the aggregation operator.

summary: This rule performs a bottom-up traversal of the logical plan tree to derive and attach statistical metadata (e.g., cardinality, data size) to each plan node using a StatsCalculator, facilitating cost-based optimization without altering the logical structure.
ra_condition: Stats(E) is null OR deepDerive is true
correctness_conditions: Child node statistics must be derived before parent nodes
StatsCalculator estimation formulas must accurately reflect data distribution
Base table statistics (catalog) must be current for scan nodes
performance_impact: Critical for CBO to select efficient join orders and access paths; introduces compilation overhead proportional to plan tree size; stale statistics may cause suboptimal plan selection.
edge_cases: Table Valued Functions (TVF) where stats depend on function parameters
Empty relations or OneRow relations with fixed cardinality
Plans with existing stats when deepDerive is false (skips recalculation)
Inconsistent deepDerive logic in DeferMaterializeTopN vs TopN
framework_notes: Implements Visitor pattern on Plan tree; operates before cost calculation; does not transform Memo groups or insert Enforcers; supports deepDerive mode to force recalculation.

summary: This rule reorders a join tree where a top-level inner join connects a lower join result with a single-row AssertNumRows operator. It pushes the top join's condition down to associate the AssertNumRows branch with one of the lower join's children, enabling earlier filtering of intermediate results.
ra_condition: T is LogicalAssertNumRows (EQ 1), Top join is Inner/Cross with single non-equi condition p, p references R or S
correctness_conditions: Join type must be Inner or Cross to preserve associativity semantics
AssertNumRows must guarantee exactly one row (Assertion.EQ, DesiredNumOfRows=1)
Join condition columns must be fully contained within the target child branch (left or right of bottom join)
LogicalProject aliases must be pushed down to ensure column visibility for the join condition
performance_impact: Beneficial when the join condition p is selective, allowing filtering of large intermediate results from the bottom join before the second join occurs. Neutral or slightly overhead if the AssertNumRows branch is already broadcast efficiently without reordering.
edge_cases: Outer joins (Left/Right/Full) are explicitly excluded
Top join with multiple OtherJoinConjuncts is not supported
Top join with HashJoinConjuncts (equi-join) is not supported
AssertNumRows asserting row count other than 1 is ignored
Condition slots not available in either bottom join child prevents transformation
framework_notes: Operates on LogicalJoin GroupExpressions within the Cascades memo. Does not insert Physical Enforcers as it is a Logical Equivalence Transformation. Handles LogicalProject wrappers to maintain slot visibility during tree restructuring.

summary: This class is a Factory pattern implementation used to instantiate and register RBO (Rule-Based Optimizer) rules within the Doris Cascades framework, rather than performing a direct relational transformation itself. The provided snippet is empty, indicating it serves as a structural placeholder or configuration point for rule creation during optimizer initialization.
ra_condition: Optimizer initialization phase
correctness_conditions: Must correctly instantiate concrete RewriteRule classes
Must not introduce semantic changes during rule creation
Must align with Cascades Memo structure expectations
performance_impact: Negligible runtime impact as it operates during optimizer setup; ensures correct rule ordering which indirectly affects query planning performance.
edge_cases: Empty rule lists if factory logic is incomplete
Circular dependencies if rules are registered incorrectly
Version mismatches between factory and concrete rule implementations
framework_notes: Does not trigger Enforcer insertion directly. Does not control Memo explore depth; it populates the RuleSet used by the Explorer. Matches no group expressions as it is not a transformation rule.

summary: This rule pushes LogicalProject operators down through LogicalJoin, LogicalWindow, LogicalPartitionTopN, and LogicalUnion operators to prune unnecessary columns early. It also optimizes Join inputs by pushing down columns required by join conjuncts even without an explicit parent Project.
ra_condition: req(R) = (out ∩ R) ∪ (cond ∩ R), req(S) = (out ∩ S) ∪ (cond ∩ S)
correctness_conditions: Columns required by the parent projection must be preserved in child projections
Columns referenced in join conditions must be preserved in child projections
For Union operators, the qualifier must be ALL (UNION ALL)
Session variable enablePruneNestedColumns must be enabled for default pushdown through nested types
Outer join semantics must be preserved (null-supplying columns handled correctly)
performance_impact: Significantly reduces I/O and memory usage by reading and processing fewer columns, especially beneficial for wide tables in columnar storage. Minimal CPU overhead for slot mapping. May increase plan complexity slightly.
edge_cases: Outer joins require careful handling to ensure null-producing rows are not incorrectly pruned
Complex nested types may be skipped if enablePruneNestedColumns is false
UNION DISTINCT cannot have project pushed through in this implementation (requires ALL)
Join conjuncts using PreferPushDownProject markers trigger specific expression rewriting logic
framework_notes: Implemented in Doris Nereids optimizer (Cascades framework). Matches GroupExpressions via PatternMatcher (e.g., logicalProject(logicalJoin())). Does not typically trigger Enforcer insertion as it preserves distribution. Controls transformation via RuleType enums and checks session variables before applying.

summary: Flattens nested LogicalExcept operators into a single n-ary LogicalExcept operator when qualifiers match, reducing plan tree depth and node count.
ra_condition: Qualifiers of parent and child Except operators are identical
correctness_conditions: Qualifiers (e.g., DISTINCT/ALL semantics) must match between parent and child Except nodes
Output schemas (SlotReferences) of all children must be union-compatible
Set difference associativity must hold for the specific qualifier configuration
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces plan tree height and operator count, lowering execution initialization overhead and simplifying subsequent optimization passes. Unlikely to hurt performance.
edge_cases: Queries with mismatched Except qualifiers (e.g. DISTINCT vs ALL) will not trigger this rule
Schemas must be union-compatible for the set operation to be valid
Does not apply if the child is not an Except operator
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions in the Memo where a LogicalExcept has a LogicalExcept as its first child. Does not trigger physical Enforcer insertion. Simplifies the logical graph to reduce search space depth and memo group complexity.

summary: Prunes empty partitions from LogicalOlapScan operations, replacing the scan with an empty relation if all selected partitions are empty, or updating the partition list to exclude empty ones.
ra_condition: Partitions in P_selected \ P_non_empty contain no visible rows
correctness_conditions: Partition metadata accurately reflects visible row counts
Transaction visibility rules are respected for txn loads (invisible sub-transaction data)
Output schema of LogicalEmptyRelation matches the original scan output
performance_impact: Significant performance gain by avoiding I/O on empty partitions. Reduces query latency for sparse data distributions. Minimal overhead for metadata checks.
edge_cases: Transaction loads where empty partitions may contain invisible data
Tables with stale partition statistics
Non-OlapTable types (rule only matches LogicalOlapScan)
framework_notes: Matches LogicalOlapScan group expressions in the Memo. Can transform the expression to LogicalEmptyRelation. Does not trigger physical Enforcer insertion. Reduces exploration depth by collapsing empty branches.

summary: This rule visits Logical CTE Consumer nodes to accumulate the set of output slots required by consumers into the statement context, enabling downstream CTE column pruning.
ra_condition: Operator = LogicalCTEConsumer
correctness_conditions: CTE ID must be valid and registered in statement context
ProducerToConsumerOutputMap must be populated prior to this rule
Must execute before CTE column pruning transformations
performance_impact: Enables CTE column pruning to reduce materialization and transmission costs; optimization overhead is minimal (map updates).
edge_cases: Recursive CTEs might have complex dependency graphs
CTEs with no consumers will not trigger this rule
Multiple consumers for same CTE aggregate slot requirements correctly
framework_notes: Matches LogicalCTEConsumer group expressions; modifies StatementContext side-effectively without altering Memo structure or triggering enforcers.

summary: This rule validates data type compatibility in join predicates and ensures no unsupported data types exist in the expression tree, acting as a validation step that returns the original plan unchanged if checks pass.
ra_condition: All join conjuncts have compatible left/right types AND no expression contains UnsupportedType
correctness_conditions: Join predicate columns must have compatible data types (leftType.acceptsType(rightType))
All expressions in the plan must not contain UnsupportedType instances
Complex types (Array, Map, Struct) must recursively contain supported types
performance_impact: No impact on query execution performance as the plan structure is unchanged. Introduces minor compilation overhead for tree traversal. Prevents runtime errors which would be significantly more costly.
edge_cases: Joins between incompatible primitive types (e.g., INT and STRING) without explicit casts
Nested complex types containing UnsupportedType
Mark Joins where hash conjuncts are empty but mark conjuncts have type mismatches
framework_notes: Although categorized as a transformation rule in the framework, this rule functions as a validator (Identity Transformation). It recursively traverses the Plan and Expression trees. It throws AnalysisException on failure, halting the optimization process. It does not generate alternative GroupExpressions or trigger Enforcer insertion.

summary: ExpressionChecker is a validation rule that traverses expression trees to verify all data types are supported by Nereids optimizer. It recursively checks expression types including nested types (Array, Map, Struct) and throws AnalysisException for unsupported types.
ra_input_pattern: N/A - This is a validation pass, not a transformation rule
ra_condition: N/A - No transformation conditions apply
correctness_conditions: All expression data types must be bound (no UnboundException)
All nested type components must be recursively validated
Unsupported types must be detected before query execution
Type checking must complete before optimization phases proceed
performance_impact: This rule has minimal performance impact as it's a validation pass. It prevents costly failures later in compilation by catching unsupported types early. No query execution performance change since it doesn't transform the plan.
edge_cases: Unbound expressions (caught and ignored via UnboundException)
Deeply nested complex types (Array of Map of Struct)
Custom or user-defined types not in UNSUPPORTED_TYPE list
Expressions during early planning phases before type resolution
framework_notes: This is not a Cascades transformation rule. It's a visitor pattern validation pass that doesn't trigger Enforcer insertion or modify Memo groups. It runs during semantic analysis phase before optimization rules apply. Uses Expression.accept() visitor pattern for tree traversal.

summary: Transforms a LogicalApply operator representing a scalar subquery into a LogicalJoin operator, distinguishing between correlated and uncorrelated subqueries to enable standard join optimization strategies.
ra_output_pattern: R × S | R ⟕ S | R ⋉ S
ra_condition: Input operator is Scalar Apply; correlated predicates must be equality
correctness_conditions: Uncorrelated right side must return exactly one row (enforced by AssertNumRows)
Correlated predicates must be equality comparisons (EqualTo)
Join type must preserve null semantics for missing scalar values in correlated case
performance_impact: Enables hash/merge joins instead of nested loops; may add AssertNumRows overhead for uncorrelated subqueries to enforce scalar constraints
edge_cases: Subqueries returning multiple rows violate scalar semantics (handled by AssertNumRows)
Non-equality correlation predicates are rejected with AnalysisException
Correlation filter eliminated by prior rules may force uncorrelated path
framework_notes: Matches LogicalApply group expressions in Cascades; triggers during logical optimization phase; produces LogicalJoin which will require distribution enforcement later

summary: This rule pushes EncodeString operations from a LogicalProject down to its child plan to compress string data early, reducing intermediate data size. It rewrites the project to reference the encoded slot directly or apply a Decode operation if the original raw value is still needed.
ra_condition: Session variable enableCompressMaterialize is true, S is a pass-through slot from child, child is not LogicalLeaf or LogicalRepeat
correctness_conditions: The Encode and Decode functions must be semantic inverses for the data type
The encoded slot must originate from a single child plan (pass-through)
If the raw slot is referenced elsewhere in the project, a Decode expression must replace it
performance_impact: Beneficial for reducing memory footprint and network transmission costs by compressing strings before materialization or shuffle. May hurt performance if CPU overhead for encoding/decoding outweighs IO savings, or if the raw data is immediately needed for computation in the same layer (which this rule avoids).
edge_cases: Encode expressions wrapping computed values (e.g., encode(A+B)) are not pushed
If the raw slot is used in a complex expression within the same Project (e.g., A+1), encoding is skipped
Disabled if session variable enableCompressMaterialize is false
Does not apply if child plan is LogicalRepeat or LogicalLeaf
framework_notes: Implemented as a Cascades RBO rule using a fluent builder pattern. It relies on ConnectContext for session variable checks and uses a custom PushDownContext and EncodeSlotPushDownVisitor to manage state and transform the child plan within the Memo structure.

summary: This rule validates LogicalAggregate nodes containing multiple DISTINCT aggregate functions, enforcing constraints on function support and column arities to ensure the query is executable by the Doris engine.
ra_condition: F contains aggregate functions with DISTINCT modifier
correctness_conditions: If multiple distinct arguments exist globally, all distinct functions must implement SupportMultiDistinct interface
Multi-column distinct functions (arity > 1 excluding order expressions) cannot coexist with multiple distinct aggregate functions
Single multi-column distinct function is allowed if it is the only distinct function in the aggregate
performance_impact: Compile-time validation only; prevents runtime failures or undefined behavior for unsupported multi-distinct queries without affecting execution performance of valid queries.
edge_cases: Queries mixing COUNT(DISTINCT col1, col2) with SUM(DISTINCT col3)
Distinct functions that do not implement SupportMultiDistinct interface used in multi-distinct context
GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT col, separator) where separator is an OrderExpression
framework_notes: Registered as RuleType.CHECK_ANALYSIS, matches LogicalAggregate group expressions in the Memo, does not transform plan structure but throws AnalysisException on violation, runs during logical analysis phase before physical optimization.

summary: This rule pushes string encoding operations down through Logical Project operators. It substitutes raw slot references with encoded slot references in the projection list and propagates the encoding expression to the child plan, optimizing data representation for downstream operators and potentially canceling redundant decode/encode pairs.
ra_input_pattern: π_{L}(P) where L contains slots S_raw and Context requires encode(S_raw) -> S_enc
ra_output_pattern: π_{L'}(P') where L' contains S_enc and P' computes S_enc from S_raw
ra_condition: Context contains encode aliases matching slots referenced in projection list L
correctness_conditions: Encoding function is deterministic and reversible (if decode exists upstream)
Downstream operators expect the encoded slot type and semantics
Slot references in project match the slots in the encode context exactly or via simple alias mapping
Data types of slots are compatible with the encoding function
performance_impact: Beneficial when encoded data reduces I/O size or matches storage format, allowing predicates to apply on encoded data. Cancels redundant decode-encode cycles (Case 3). May incur CPU overhead if encoding is pushed to rows that are later filtered out, but context usually implies necessity.
edge_cases: Complex expressions in project (e.g., a + b) wrapped in encode (Case 4)
Decode followed by Encode which cancels out to direct slot reference (Case 3)
Slots referenced in project not present in the pushdown context (no change)
LogicalRepeat operator handled separately to ensure aliases are projected
framework_notes: Implemented as a Visitor in Doris Cascades framework. Uses PushDownContext to carry encode aliases down the tree. Modifies LogicalProject nodes by rewriting projections and recursively visiting children with updated context. Does not trigger physical Enforcers directly but prepares logical plan for storage encoding optimizations.

summary: This rule infers implicit IS NOT NULL constraints from existing filter predicates (e.g., col = 1 implies col IS NOT NULL) and explicitly adds them to the filter operator to assist downstream optimization phases.
ra_condition: Q = inferNotNull(P) AND Q ⊄ P
correctness_conditions: Inferred predicates Q must be logically implied by existing predicates P (P ⇒ Q)
Adding redundant predicates must not alter the result set semantics
Inference must respect join types and nullability context (handled by ExpressionUtils)
performance_impact: Beneficial for cardinality estimation and enabling null-aware optimization strategies in downstream operators (joins, aggregations); evaluation cost of IS NOT NULL is negligible.
edge_cases: Filter already contains the inferred IS NOT NULL predicates
Columns are defined NOT NULL in schema metadata (redundant but harmless)
Complex expressions where nullability inference is ambiguous or unsupported
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter group expressions; prevents infinite loops by checking isGeneratedIsNotNull flag on existing conjuncts; returns null if no new predicates are generated to avoid unnecessary memoization.

summary: Normalizes predicate expressions in LogicalFilter and LogicalJoin operators to ensure they explicitly return the Boolean type via casting.
ra_condition: type(p) ≠ Boolean
correctness_conditions: Expression p must be semantically coercible to Boolean
Casting must preserve truth-value semantics (e.g., non-zero to true)
Expression evaluation must not have side effects altered by casting
performance_impact: Negligible CPU cost for explicit casts; prevents runtime type inference overhead; ensures consistency for downstream optimization rules expecting strict Boolean types.
edge_cases: Predicates already returning Boolean type (no-op)
Expressions with types incompatible with Boolean coercion
NULL value handling during type coercion
framework_notes: Visitor-based RBO rule. Modifies Plan expressions in-place within Memo groups. Does not change tree topology or trigger Enforcers. Matches LogicalFilter and LogicalJoin group expressions during logical optimization phase.

summary: This rule performs partition pruning on LogicalOlapScan nodes by analyzing filter predicates or manually specified tablet IDs to restrict the scan to relevant partitions, potentially replacing the scan with an empty relation if no partitions match.
ra_condition: R is a partitioned table scan, p references partition columns OR R has manually specified tablet constraints
correctness_conditions: Table represented by R must be a partitioned OlapTable
Partition pruning logic must guarantee no false negatives (no matching rows excluded)
Mapping between Tablet IDs and Partition IDs must be accurate
Predicate evaluation against partition bounds must be semantically correct
performance_impact: Highly beneficial for large partitioned tables by reducing I/O and data processed. Overhead is limited to optimization time. Can eliminate entire query execution if result is empty.
edge_cases: Non-partitioned tables (rule does not match)
Filters on non-partition columns (no pruning possible)
Session variable skipPrunePredicate is enabled
Manually specified tablet IDs spanning multiple partitions
Complex predicates that cannot be analyzed for range pruning
framework_notes: Matches LogicalOlapScan or LogicalFilter->LogicalOlapScan group expressions. Transforms LogicalOlapScan properties (selectedPartitionIds). Can produce LogicalEmptyRelation. Applied during Logical Optimization (RBO) phase in Cascades.

summary: This rule merges LogicalOneRowRelation children into a Union operator by converting them to constant expressions stored in the Union itself, reducing the number of union branches and simplifying the query plan structure.
ra_condition: Union has at least one LogicalOneRowRelation child without multiple unique functions
correctness_conditions: Type coercion must be applied when OneRow expression types don't match Union output types
OneRowRelations containing unique functions that exist multiple times must not be merged
All expressions must maintain semantic equivalence after merging
Output slot references must be preserved correctly for remaining children
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces union branch count, simplifies execution plan, eliminates unnecessary OneRowRelation operators, reduces memory overhead. Potential downside: Minimal, as constant expressions are cheaper than separate plan nodes and the transformation is semantically equivalent.
edge_cases: OneRowRelations with unique functions appearing multiple times (handled by AddProjectForUniqueFunction rule instead)
Unions where all children are already non-OneRow relations (no transformation occurs)
Type mismatches requiring complex coercion between OneRow expressions and Union output schema
Empty Union or Union with single child (arity checks prevent invalid transformations)
framework_notes: This is an RBO (Rule-Based Optimization) logical equivalence transformation rule in Doris Cascades framework. It matches Union group expressions containing LogicalOneRowRelation children. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion as it's a logical transformation. Reduces memo explore depth by eliminating child plan nodes and converting them to constant expressions. Matches group expressions where Union has OneRowRelation as any child.

summary: This rule simplifies LogicalProject expressions by canceling out redundant pairs of EncodeString and DecodeAsVarchar functions when the session variable for compress materialize is enabled.
ra_condition: Session variable enableCompressMaterialize is true, f and g are inverse encode/decode functions
correctness_conditions: EncodeString and DecodeAsVarchar must be semantic inverses
Null handling semantics must be preserved during cancellation
Session variable ConnectContext.enableCompressMaterialize must be enabled
Implementation must correctly flag project update when changes occur
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing CPU overhead associated with unnecessary serialization and deserialization operations during query execution. No negative performance impact expected unless the tags were used for downstream optimization hints.
edge_cases: Rule does not trigger if enableCompressMaterialize session variable is false
Only applies to Alias expressions within LogicalProject, ignores other NamedExpression types
Implementation bug: If all projections are optimized, hasNewProjections remains false and the optimized plan is not returned
Does not handle Encode/Decode pairs separated by other operators
framework_notes: Matches LogicalProject group expressions in the Memo. It is an RBO rule that rewrites the logical plan without triggering physical Enforcer insertion. It iterates through projection list to transform expression trees.

summary: Pushes a TopN operator below a Join operator when the TopN follows a Distinct Aggregation, reducing the number of rows processed by the join under specific safety conditions.
ra_condition: ⋈ ∈ {⟕, ⟖, ×}, G ⊇ output(R), order_keys ⊆ output(R) as prefix
correctness_conditions: Join type must be Left Outer, Right Outer, or Cross Join (Inner Join excluded)
Distinct aggregation group-by columns must be a superset of the pushed child's output columns
TopN order-by keys available in the pushed child must form a prefix of the total order-by keys
TopN limit plus offset must not exceed the session variable threshold (topnOptLimitThreshold)
Order-by expressions must be simple Slot references
performance_impact: Beneficial when it significantly reduces the number of rows entering the join operator; may incur overhead if the pushed TopN/Distinct operations are costly or if limit threshold is high.
edge_cases: Inner Joins or Full Outer Joins
Complex order-by expressions (non-Slot)
Distinct columns do not cover all output columns of the join child
TopN limit exceeds configured optimization threshold
Order-by keys from child do not appear as a prefix in the total order-by list
framework_notes: RBO rule in Doris Cascades optimizer; matches LogicalTopN -> LogicalAggregate (Distinct) -> LogicalJoin pattern; returns null if transformation yields no structural change or violates safety checks; does not explicitly trigger enforcers but modifies logical plan structure.

summary: Pushes filter predicates down through a Join operator to child relations where semantically safe, reducing join input size, and converts eligible equality predicates into join conditions for Inner Joins.
ra_condition: p_left refs R only, p_right refs S only, JoinType supports pushdown
correctness_conditions: For Outer Joins, predicates on the null-supplying side cannot be pushed below the join to preserve null-extended rows
Predicates referencing columns from both sides remain on top unless they are equalities convertible to join conditions (Inner Join only)
Predicates containing UDFs (UniqueFunctions) are not pushed down to ensure safety and determinism
Constant predicates (no slot references) are pushed to both children
performance_impact: Generally beneficial by reducing cardinality early; reduces network shuffle and join computation cost. Minimal risk unless filter evaluation cost exceeds join savings.
edge_cases: Outer joins: filtering null-supplying side below join changes semantics to Inner Join
Predicates referencing columns from both inputs cannot be pushed to children unless converted to join conditions
ASOF joins have specific pushdown restrictions defined in COULD_PUSH_THROUGH lists
Predicates with UDFs remain at the top of the join
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter(LogicalJoin) pattern in Cascades framework. Uses PlanUtils.filterOrSelf to prune empty filters. Classifies predicates into left, right, remaining, and join conditions based on slot references and JoinType allowlists.

summary: This rule eliminates redundant LogicalAggregate (Group By) operators when the grouping columns are already unique and not null in the child plan, rewriting aggregate functions to simple expressions or constants.
ra_condition: G is unique and NOT NULL in P
correctness_conditions: Grouping columns must be unique in the child plan (one row per group)
Grouping columns must be NOT NULL in the child plan
All aggregate functions must be supported for non-aggregate rewrite (e.g., Sum, Count, Min, Max)
Null handling semantics must be preserved during expression rewrite (e.g., Count(col) becomes IF(ISNULL(col), 0, 1))
performance_impact: Beneficial: Removes hashing/sorting overhead and aggregation logic, reducing CPU and memory usage. Neutral/Negative: Minimal overhead for rule matching; relies on accurate trait propagation from child plans.
edge_cases: Unsupported aggregate functions (e.g., Group_Concat, Hll, UDAFs not in supported sets)
Group By expressions are not simple Slots (e.g., expressions or functions)
Child plan lacks LogicalProperties/Traits indicating uniqueness
Complex null handling requirements for specific functions like Stddev or Sum0
framework_notes: Cascades RBO rule matching LogicalAggregate. Checks if GroupBy expressions are Slots. Verifies uniqueness via child LogicalProperties/Traits. Rewrites to Project operator. RuleType: ELIMINATE_GROUP_BY.

summary: This rule traverses the logical plan tree to identify which columns (slots) are actively used by upstream operators. It propagates this usage information down to scan operators, allowing them to prune unused columns from their output schema, thereby reducing I/O and network overhead.
ra_condition: Schema(R') ⊆ Schema(R) and R' contains all columns required for A and mandatory keys
correctness_conditions: All columns referenced in upper expressions (projections, predicates, joins) must be included in the operative set
For Unique, Aggregate, or Primary key tables, key columns must always be included to ensure row identification correctness
Virtual columns must be included if they are referenced in the query
Union branches must produce consistent columns; if one branch needs a slot, all branches must provide it
performance_impact: Significantly reduces disk I/O and network shuffle traffic by reading and transferring only necessary columns. The optimization overhead is linear with respect to the plan tree size.
edge_cases: SELECT * queries result in no pruning as all columns are marked operative
Tables with Unique/Primary keys have limited pruning potential as key columns are forced
Queries with complex expressions or UDFs may obscure column dependencies
Union operations require alignment of operative slots across all child branches
framework_notes: Implements a top-down PlanVisitor to collect operative slot IDs in a context. It modifies leaf scan nodes by calling withOperativeSlots. It operates on Logical Plans before physical planning, does not trigger Enforcer insertion, and ensures consistency across Union branches via back-propagation.

summary: Pushes filter predicates down through a Window operator when the predicates reference only the columns used as partition keys, reducing the data volume processed by the window function.
ra_condition: Columns referenced in P_pushed are a subset of partition keys K
correctness_conditions: The filter predicate must reference only columns that define the window partitioning.
Filtering on partition keys ensures that entire partitions are either kept or removed, preserving window semantics within retained partitions.
Partition keys should ideally be simple column references, not expressions, to ensure safe predicate evaluation.
performance_impact: Beneficial when predicates are selective, reducing the number of rows sorted or hashed for window calculation. Negligible overhead if no predicates are pushed.
edge_cases: Window partition keys defined by expressions (e.g., c1+c2) prevent pushing down filters on constituent columns (e.g., c1=1).
Filters referencing columns not in the partition key remain above the window.
Multiple window functions with different partition keys may result in an empty common partition key set, preventing pushdown.
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter atop LogicalWindow in the Cascades memo. Returns null if no predicates can be pushed, signaling no transformation. Does not directly handle physical enforcers.

summary: This rule pushes filter predicates down below a logical aggregation operator when the predicates reference only grouping columns and do not contain unique functions, reducing the data volume processed by the aggregation.
ra_condition: slots(p_pushed) ⊆ g AND p_pushed contains no unique functions
correctness_conditions: Predicates pushed down must reference only columns present in the GROUP BY clause
Predicates must not contain unique or volatile functions (e.g., random(), now())
Predicates must reference at least one slot (no constant-only predicates)
Semantics of aggregation must remain unchanged by early filtering
performance_impact: Generally beneficial as it reduces the cardinality of input to the expensive aggregation operator, lowering memory and CPU consumption. Overhead is minimal.
edge_cases: Predicates referencing aggregated results (e.g., HAVING clause conditions on SUM(col))
Predicates referencing non-grouping columns not in the output
Predicates containing non-deterministic functions
Queries with Grouping Sets (handled via SourceRepeat logic)
framework_notes: Implements a Logical Equivalence Transformation in Doris Cascades optimizer. Matches LogicalFilter child of LogicalAggregate. Modifies Memo group expressions by creating a new LogicalFilter below the Aggregate.

summary: Eliminates redundant Sort operators that appear directly beneath Subquery Alias or View operators in the logical plan tree.
ra_condition: ρ represents SubqueryAlias or View operator; τ is Sort operator; R is the underlying relation
correctness_conditions: SQL standard semantics do not guarantee row order preservation through subqueries or views unless LIMIT is present
The Sort operator is not functionally required by a co-located LIMIT or TOP-N operator
Logical plan operators do not enforce physical ordering properties
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces CPU and memory consumption by avoiding unnecessary sorting operations. No negative impact as required ordering is enforced during physical optimization if needed.
edge_cases: Subqueries or Views that include LIMIT clauses (order matters for result set)
Non-standard SQL dialects relying on implicit order preservation
Plans where Sort is required for downstream Merge Join (handled by physical enforcers)
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions with SubQueryAlias/View root and Sort child in the Memo. Purely logical transformation; does not trigger Enforcer insertion. Reduces tree height for subsequent exploration.

summary: Pushes filter predicates comparing MAX/MIN aggregate results to literals down to the aggregate's input, converting them to predicates on the underlying column to reduce data volume before aggregation.
ra_condition: func ∈ {MAX, MIN}, single agg func, group by ≠ ∅, op compatible with func (MAX: >/>=, MIN: </<=)
correctness_conditions: Aggregate must contain exactly one aggregate function
The aggregate function must be MAX or MIN
Filter predicate must compare the aggregate output slot to a literal value
Comparison operator must match function direction (MAX: >/>=, MIN: </<=)
Aggregate must have non-empty grouping expressions
performance_impact: Reduces data volume entering the aggregation operator when the predicate is selective. Generally beneficial with negligible overhead.
edge_cases: Global aggregates (empty group by) are explicitly excluded by rule logic
Aggregates with multiple functions or non-MAX/MIN functions are ignored
Complex predicates (non-literal, non-slot reference) are not supported
NULL values are handled by standard SQL comparison semantics
framework_notes: Logical RBO rule matching Filter-Aggregate pattern. Replaces group expression in Memo. Does not trigger physical enforcers. Requires specific conjunct structure.

summary: Pushes Limit operator down through Join, Window, and Union operators to reduce intermediate row counts early in the plan, subject to semantic preservation constraints like zero offset and specific join types.
ra_condition: offset=0, \bowtie \in \{⟕, ⟖, \times\}, \cup = \text{UNION ALL}
correctness_conditions: Limit offset must be zero to preserve global skipping semantics
Join type must be Left Outer, Right Outer, or Cross; Inner Join is explicitly excluded
Union operator must be UNION ALL (Qualifier.ALL) to preserve distinct semantics
Window functions must support partition limiting (e.g., ROW_NUMBER)
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing data volume early, lowering memory usage and network shuffle costs. May be neutral if the limit is not selective or if pushed to both sides of a Cross Join increases overhead.
edge_cases: Queries with LIMIT offset > 0 (rule does not fire)
Inner Joins (transformation returns null)
UNION DISTINCT operations (rule checks Qualifier.ALL)
Window functions that do not support partition limit pushdown
framework_notes: Implemented in Doris Cascades RBO. Uses pattern matching on LogicalPlan (logicalLimit, logicalJoin, etc.). Includes a MergeLimits rule. Checks for plan equality to prevent redundant transformations.

summary: This rule transforms a query plan where a Left Semi or Anti Join is applied on top of an Aggregate operation. It swaps their order so the Aggregate is applied after the Join, allowing the Join to be reordered with operations below the original Aggregate position. This transformation is only applied if the Join condition relies solely on the Aggregate's grouping columns.
ra_condition: θ references only columns in G, JoinType ∈ {LeftSemi, LeftAnti}
correctness_conditions: Join condition columns must be a subset of the Aggregate's grouping columns
Join type must be Left Semi or Left Anti Join
Aggregate must not be a scalar aggregation (must have grouping columns)
Join must not be a Mark Join
Session variable disable_join_reorder must be false
performance_impact: Beneficial when it enables join reordering with other tables below the aggregate, allowing earlier filtering. Potentially harmful if the aggregate significantly reduces cardinality before the join, as delaying aggregation increases join input size.
edge_cases: Scalar aggregation (no GROUP BY clause)
Join predicates referencing aggregated or non-grouping columns
Mark Joins
Query sessions with join reordering disabled
framework_notes: Matches LogicalJoin with LogicalAggregate on the left child in the Cascades Memo. Generates a new Logical expression allowing the optimizer to explore plans where the join occurs before aggregation. Does not directly handle physical Enforcers.

summary: This rule rewrites join predicates containing CASE WHEN (or IF) expressions that reference columns from both join sides. It extracts these expressions into disjunctive normal form (OR expansion) or one-sided predicates to enable further optimizations like predicate pushdown or partition pruning.
ra_condition: θ contains CASE WHEN/IF expressions referencing slots from both R and S, and join type supports rewrite (not ASOF)
correctness_conditions: The rewritten predicate θ' must be semantically equivalent to θ
NULL handling in CASE WHEN expressions must be preserved
Join type must support predicate extraction (e.g., INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, but not ASOF)
Expressions must not contain unique functions (UDFs) that prevent rewriting
performance_impact: Beneficial when it enables partition pruning or index usage on expanded terms. Can be detrimental if the OR expansion creates too many disjuncts, increasing planning time or execution complexity without selectivity gains.
edge_cases: ASOF joins are explicitly excluded
Mark joins have limited rewriting on hash conjuncts
Expressions containing UDFs (unique functions) are skipped
Non-EQUAL predicates do not trigger OrExpansion logic
Case When expressions referencing only one side are ignored
framework_notes: Implemented as a Cascades RBO rule matching LogicalJoin group expressions. It modifies join conjuncts directly using ExpressionRewriteContext. It interacts with OrExpansion and PushDownJoinOtherCondition utilities to determine validity. It does not trigger Enforcer insertion directly but prepares predicates for downstream rules. It adds transformed expressions to the Memo via the transformation.

summary: This rule performs a bottom-up traversal of the logical plan tree to compute and attach statistical metadata (row count, data size, etc.) to each operator node using a StatsCalculator, enabling cost-based optimization without altering the logical plan structure.
ra_condition: Statistics(Op) == null OR deepDerive == true
correctness_conditions: Child node statistics must be fully derived before parent node statistics are computed
Statistical formulas in StatsCalculator must accurately model the cardinality and size changes of each operator
Base relation statistics (Scans) must be available from the catalog or estimated defaults
performance_impact: Critical for Cost-Based Optimization (CBO) to estimate plan costs and choose optimal join orders; introduces compilation overhead linear to the plan tree size; deepDerive mode forces recalculation increasing latency
edge_cases: LogicalDeferMaterializeTopN uses '&&' instead of '||' for condition check, potentially skipping derivation when stats are null but deepDerive is false
LogicalAssertNumRows implementation appears truncated in the provided source code
TVF relations compute stats dynamically via function definition rather than calculator
Empty relations and OneRow relations have specialized stat computation logic
framework_notes: Implements Visitor pattern on Plan tree rather than matching Memo GroupExpressions; does not transform Memo structure or insert Physical Enforcers; operates as a metadata enrichment pass before cost estimation; generic visit method defaults to passing last child's stats, overridden by specific operators like Join and Aggregate

summary: This rule optimizes Correlated Apply operators by splitting filter predicates on the right child. It pushes uncorrelated predicates down to the right child's input and merges correlated predicates into the Apply operator's condition, reducing data volume in the correlation loop.
ra_condition: p_corr is not empty; p_uncorr references only columns from R
correctness_conditions: Uncorrelated predicates must not reference correlation slots from the left side
Correlated predicates must be correctly identified via splitCorrelatedConjuncts
Apply node semantics (e.g., Mark Join, Outer/Inner) must be preserved during reconstruction
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces the number of rows processed in the correlated subquery for each outer row by filtering early. Neutral: If no uncorrelated predicates exist, it simply absorbs the Filter node into the Apply.
edge_cases: Filters containing only uncorrelated predicates (rule skips, handled by other pushdown rules)
Filters containing only correlated predicates (results in Filter elimination)
Complex expressions where correlation dependency is ambiguous
framework_notes: Cascades RBO rule matching LogicalApply with LogicalFilter right child. Triggers only if Apply is correlated and Filter contains at least one correlated predicate. Operates on Memo group expressions without physical enforcers.

summary: This class implements the Factory design pattern within the Doris Nereids optimizer to instantiate specific logical rewrite rules. It does not contain transformation logic itself but delegates rule creation to concrete implementations during optimizer initialization. Consequently, it serves as a structural component for rule management rather than a direct relational algebra transformation.
ra_input_pattern: N/A (Factory Class does not match relational expressions)
ra_output_pattern: N/A (Factory Class does not produce relational expressions)
ra_condition: N/A (Instantiation occurs during optimizer setup, not query exploration)
correctness_conditions: Instantiated rules must preserve semantic equivalence
Factory must correctly configure rule parameters
Rule registration must not introduce cyclic dependencies
performance_impact: Negligible impact on query execution; overhead is limited to optimizer initialization phase.
edge_cases: Misconfiguration during factory instantiation
Rules created may not match intended plan patterns
Empty factory yields no active rules
framework_notes: Does not trigger Enforcer insertion directly; controls rule availability rather than Memo explore depth. Matches no group expressions as it operates at the framework configuration level.

summary: Flattens nested LogicalExcept operators into a single n-ary LogicalExcept node when the set qualifiers (DISTINCT/ALL) match, reducing tree depth.
ra_condition: Qualifier(Parent) == Qualifier(Child)
correctness_conditions: Both Except operators must have the same Qualifier (ALL or DISTINCT)
All child plans must be union-compatible (same column count and types)
Semantics must treat n-ary Except as First minus Union(Rest)
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing plan tree depth and potentially consolidating shuffle/exchange operations; unlikely to hurt performance as it is a logical simplification.
edge_cases: Nested Excepts with different Qualifiers (e.g., DISTINCT wrapping ALL)
Schemas where child outputs are not union-compatible
Single child Except operators (invalid structure)
framework_notes: Operates on LogicalPlan GroupExpressions in the Memo; does not trigger Physical Enforcers; reduces exploration depth by simplifying the tree structure early in RBO phase.

summary: This rule validates data type compatibility across the logical plan tree, ensuring join keys match and no unsupported types exist, acting as a gatekeeper without modifying the plan structure.
ra_input_pattern: P (Composition of σ, π, ⋈, γ, τ, etc.)
ra_output_pattern: P (Composition of σ, π, ⋈, γ, τ, etc.)
ra_condition: ∀ (R ⋈_q S) ⊆ P, type(q.left) accepts type(q.right) AND ∀ expr ∈ P, type(expr) ∉ UnsupportedType
correctness_conditions: Join conjuncts must have compatible data types on both sides (leftType.acceptsType(rightType))
Expressions must not contain UnsupportedType instances
Nested complex types (Array, Map, Struct) must be recursively valid
performance_impact: Introduces compilation overhead linear to plan size; prevents runtime analysis exceptions; no direct execution performance gain.
edge_cases: UnboundException in expressions is caught and ignored during traversal
Mark joins fallback to mark conjuncts if hash conjuncts are empty
Recursive validation for nested complex types (Array of Map, etc.)
framework_notes: Runs during logical optimization phase in Nereids; traverses Plan tree directly rather than matching Memo GroupExpressions for alternatives; does not trigger Enforcer insertion as it does not change physical properties.

summary: This component recursively validates expression data types to ensure they are supported by the Nereids optimizer, acting as a validation pass rather than a transformative optimization rule.
ra_condition: DataType(Expr) ∉ UNSUPPORTED_TYPE ∧ Expr is Bound
correctness_conditions: All expression slots must be resolved (no UnboundException thrown)
Primitive and complex types (Array, Map, Struct) must be recursively supported
Transformation is identity; semantic equivalence is preserved by definition
performance_impact: Negligible runtime impact as it is a compile-time check; prevents costly failures during execution by catching unsupported types early in the analysis phase.
edge_cases: Expressions containing UnboundSlots will skip type checking safely
Deeply nested complex types (e.g., Array<Struct<Map>>) increase recursion depth
Custom UDTs not registered in UNSUPPORTED_TYPE whitelist may cause AnalysisException
framework_notes: Implements Visitor pattern on Expression tree, not a standard Cascades Rule matching GroupExpressions. Does not modify Memo but validates nodes before/during exploration. Throws AnalysisException on failure.

summary: Pushes EncodeString expressions from a LogicalProject down to the child plan to compress string data early, rewriting the project to reference encoded slots or decode them if original values are needed.
ra_condition: enableCompressMaterialize=true, S is SlotReference passable through P, P not Leaf/Repeat
correctness_conditions: Encoding and Decoding functions are lossless and inverses
Slot S is a direct input to the project (not computed by other aliases in same project)
Child plan P can accept and compute the encoded slot
Session variable enableCompressMaterialize is enabled
performance_impact: Reduces data volume for network shuffle and intermediate materialization at the cost of CPU cycles for encoding and decoding. Beneficial for large string columns or wide shuffles.
edge_cases: Computed expressions like encode(A+B) are not pushed
Session variable enableCompressMaterialize is disabled
Child plan is LogicalLeaf or LogicalRepeat
Slot S originates from multiple children in a Join without proper mapping
framework_notes: RBO rule in Cascades framework. Matches LogicalProject group expressions. Checks session variables via ConnectContext. Uses EncodeSlotPushDownVisitor to traverse child plan. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion directly but modifies logical plan structure before physical optimization.

summary: This rule pushes EncodeString operations down through LogicalProject operators by replacing slot references with encoded slots in the projection list and propagating encoding requirements to child plans, aiming to optimize data representation early in the query pipeline.
ra_condition: encode(slot) ∈ PushDownContext ∧ slot ∈ references(exprs) ∨ slot ∈ output(Plan)
correctness_conditions: Encoding function is deterministic and preserves equality semantics
Slot references match between project expressions and child output schema
Null semantics are preserved during encoding transformation
Expression evaluation order does not change semantics when wrapped with encode
Decode/Encode pairs cancel out correctly without data loss
performance_impact: Beneficial when reducing data size early (compression) or enabling efficient operations on encoded keys (e.g., integer vs string joins). May hurt performance if encoding overhead is high and data is decoded immediately downstream or if it prevents other optimizations.
edge_cases: LogicalRepeat operators have specific handling bypassing standard pushdown
Complex expressions in projections (Case 4) wrap the expression with encode rather than pushing to child
Decode expressions in projections (Case 3) may cancel out encode requirements
Aliases that cannot be pushed result in an additional Project layer added on top
framework_notes: Implements Visitor pattern with PushDownContext state to track aliases. Modifies LogicalProject projections and recurses to children. Operates as an RBO logical equivalence transformation without triggering physical Enforcer insertion. Controls exploration via recursive visitChildren calls.

summary: This rule performs partition pruning on OLAP table scans by analyzing filter predicates and manually specified tablet IDs to reduce the number of partitions that need to be scanned, potentially replacing the scan with an empty relation if all partitions are eliminated.
ra_input_pattern: σ_p(Scan_olap(T)) OR Scan_olap(T) with tablet hints
ra_output_pattern: Scan_olap(T, pruned_partitions) OR ∅ (if all partitions pruned)
ra_condition: T is partitioned table, scan not already pruned, partition columns accessible
correctness_conditions: Table must be a partitioned OlapTable
Scan must not have been previously partition pruned (isPartitionPruned() == false)
Partition column names must be available in scan output slots
Manually specified tablet IDs must map to valid partitions
Filter predicates must be evaluable against partition column values
performance_impact: Highly beneficial when filter predicates can eliminate significant portions of partitions, reducing I/O and scan time. May have minimal overhead when no partitions can be pruned. Could hurt if pruning computation is expensive relative to small table size.
edge_cases: Non-partitioned tables (rule won't match)
Already pruned scans (isPartitionPruned() == true)
Partition columns not in scan output (pruning cannot proceed)
All partitions pruned resulting in LogicalEmptyRelation
Manually specified tablet IDs spanning multiple partitions
Session variable skipPrunePredicate enabled
framework_notes: RBO rule in Cascades framework. Matches LogicalOlapScan directly or LogicalFilter over LogicalOlapScan. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion as it's a logical equivalence transformation. Controls Memo depth by replacing scan node with pruned version in same group. Uses PartitionPruner.prune() for predicate-based pruning and prunePartitionByTabletIds() for tablet-ID based pruning.

summary: Merges LogicalOneRowRelation children into a LogicalUnion operator as constant expressions, reducing the number of child plan nodes.
ra_input_pattern: ∪(R₁, ..., Rₙ, C₁, ..., Cₘ) where Cᵢ are OneRow relations
ra_condition: Cᵢ does not contain unique functions evaluated multiple times; types are coercible
correctness_conditions: OneRow relations must not contain unique functions that exist multiple times
Data types of OneRow projections must match or be coercible to Union output schema
Union operator implementation must support constant expression lists
performance_impact: Reduces plan tree depth and node count, lowering planning and execution overhead. Beneficial when Union has many constant rows.
edge_cases: OneRow relations containing unique functions (e.g., uuid, now)
Unions where all children are OneRow relations
Type coercion failures between OneRow output and Union schema
framework_notes: Matches LogicalUnion group expressions in Nereids Cascades framework. Uses withChildrenAndConstExprsList to transform the operator. Does not trigger physical enforcers.

summary: Optimizes queries by pushing a TopN operator below a Join and Distinct Aggregate, reducing the number of rows processed by the Join and Aggregation stages for Outer and Cross Joins.
ra_condition: Join type ∈ {⟕, ⟖, ×}, Order Keys ⊆ Child Output, Group By Slots ⊇ Child Output Slots, Limit < Threshold
correctness_conditions: Join type must be Left Outer, Right Outer, or Cross Join
TopN order keys must be simple Slot references present in the target join child
Distinct/GroupBy slots must cover all output slots of the target join child
TopN limit + offset must be within the session variable topnOptLimitThreshold
Target join child must not already be a TopN operator
Pushed TopN is applied on a Distinct projection of the join child to ensure uniqueness
performance_impact: Reduces data volume for Join and Distinct operations, improving performance for large datasets with selective TopN; potential overhead if limit is large or pushdown filtering is ineffective.
edge_cases: Inner Joins and Full Outer Joins are not supported
Order By expressions containing complex logic (non-Slot)
GroupBy columns do not strictly cover all child output columns
TopN limit exceeds the configured session threshold
framework_notes: RBO rule in Cascades framework matching LogicalTopN -> LogicalAggregate -> LogicalJoin patterns; validates against ConnectContext session variables; returns null if transformation is unsafe or yields no change.

summary: This rule traverses the logical plan tree top-down to identify which columns (slots) are actually required by upstream operations (projections, predicates) and marks them as 'operative'. It then configures scan operators to only read these operative columns, effectively performing column pruning to reduce I/O.
ra_condition: R_{subset} \subseteq R, containing all columns referenced in req plus mandatory key columns
correctness_conditions: All slots referenced in upper expressions (projects, filters) must be included in the operative set
For Unique, Agg, or Primary Key tables, key columns must always be included even if not explicitly referenced to ensure row identity
Virtual columns and their input dependencies must be included if referenced
In Union operators, if a column is operative in any child branch, it must be marked operative in the Union output and all other children to maintain schema consistency
performance_impact: Significantly reduces I/O and network overhead by reading and transmitting fewer columns from storage engines. Optimization overhead is minimal as it requires a single tree traversal.
edge_cases: SELECT * queries result in no pruning as all columns are marked operative
Tables with Unique/Primary/Agg keys force key columns to be read, limiting pruning granularity even if only value columns are selected
Complex Union queries may require broader column sets due to schema alignment across branches (one branch needs a column, all must produce it)
framework_notes: RBO rule implemented as a PlanVisitor within the Cascades framework. Uses DeriveContext to pass operative slot IDs (RoaringBitmap) down the tree. Modifies LogicalScan nodes via withOperativeSlots method. Does not trigger physical property enforcers directly.

summary: Pushes filter predicates down below an aggregation operator if they reference only grouping columns and contain no unique functions, reducing aggregation input size.
ra_condition: P_push ⊆ P, slots(P_push) ⊆ G, P_push contains no unique functions, P_push is not empty
correctness_conditions: Predicates must reference only columns present in the GROUP BY clause
Predicates must not contain non-deterministic or unique functions (e.g., random())
Predicates must reference at least one column slot
Semantics must remain equivalent (filtering before vs after grouping)
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces cardinality feeding into the aggregation operator, lowering memory and CPU cost for hashing/sorting. Neutral if no predicates can be pushed.
edge_cases: HAVING clauses referencing aggregate functions (e.g., SUM(a) > 10)
Predicates referencing non-grouping columns
Predicates containing volatile functions (e.g., random(), uuid())
Constant predicates without column references
Complex grouping sets where slots are not common across all sets
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter over LogicalAggregate in Cascades Memo. Generates equivalent logical plan. Returns null if no predicates can be pushed to avoid infinite rule application loops. Does not trigger physical property enforcers.

summary: This rule eliminates LogicalSort operators nested directly under LogicalSubQueryAlias or LogicalView operators, as ordering inside a subquery or view is semantically irrelevant without a LIMIT clause.
ra_condition: Sort is pure (no LIMIT), outer query does not require specific order
correctness_conditions: The LogicalSort operator does not include a LIMIT or TOP-N constraint
SQL standard semantics treat subquery and view results as unordered multisets
No outer operator depends on the specific physical ordering produced by the inner sort
performance_impact: Beneficial: Removes unnecessary sorting overhead (CPU/Memory). Neutral: No change in result cardinality or correctness under standard SQL semantics.
edge_cases: Subqueries or Views containing ORDER BY with LIMIT (TopN) where sort determines row survival
Legacy applications relying on non-standard ordering preservation in views
Cases where sort is used for side-effects (e.g. specific execution engine behaviors)
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions with SubQueryAlias/View root and Sort child. Replaces child pointer to skip Sort. RBO rule, does not trigger physical enforcers directly. Reduces memo tree height.

summary: This rule pushes a Limit operator down through child operators such as Join, Project, Window, and Union to reduce data volume early in the execution plan. It specifically handles Left/Right/Cross Joins, Union All, and Window functions that support partition limits, while excluding Limits with non-zero offsets.
ra_condition: Join type ∈ {LEFT_OUTER, RIGHT_OUTER, CROSS}, Limit offset = 0
correctness_conditions: Limit offset must be zero (hasValidOffset is false)
Join type must allow semantic preservation (Left, Right, or Cross)
Union operator must be UNION ALL (Qualifier.ALL)
Window functions must support partition limit pushdown
No global ordering requirement violated by early truncation (implicit)
performance_impact: Beneficial for reducing intermediate data size and enabling early termination of child scans. For Union All, it may increase total rows processed before the final limit (N per branch vs N total) but allows each branch to stop early. For Cross Joins, it significantly reduces the cartesian product space.
edge_cases: Limits with non-zero offsets are ignored
Inner Joins and Full Outer Joins are not transformed
Window functions without pushdown support remain unchanged
Queries requiring specific global ordering without Sort below Limit may behave differently if order is not preserved
framework_notes: Implemented as RBO Logical Equivalence Transformation in Doris Cascades. Uses pattern matchers (logicalLimit, logicalJoin, etc.) and predicates (whenNot). Returns null if transformation yields no structural change to prevent infinite loops. Registers multiple specific rule types (PUSH_LIMIT_THROUGH_JOIN, etc.).

summary: This rule transforms join predicates containing CASE WHEN expressions that reference columns from both join sides into equivalent OR-based predicates. This exposes hidden disjunctions to enable subsequent OR Expansion optimizations, allowing complex joins to be split into unions of simpler joins.
ra_condition: θ contains CASE WHEN/IF expressions referencing slots from both R and S, join is not ASOF, and optimization context allows PushDown or OR Expansion
correctness_conditions: The transformation must preserve semantic equivalence between CASE WHEN and the generated OR/AND logic
Null handling semantics of CASE WHEN must be preserved in the expanded predicate
Join type must support predicate modification (ASOF joins are excluded)
Expressions must not contain unique/non-deterministic functions that prevent rewriting
Slot references must be valid and belong to the respective join inputs
performance_impact: Beneficial when it enables OR Expansion, allowing the optimizer to split a complex join into multiple simpler joins that can utilize indexes or better join algorithms. Potentially harmful if the expansion creates an excessive number of disjuncts, increasing planning time and plan complexity without selectivity benefits.
edge_cases: ASOF Joins are explicitly excluded from rewriting
Mark Joins handle hash conditions differently and may not extract OR expansions from them
CASE WHEN expressions referencing slots from only one side are ignored
Expressions containing unique functions are skipped to prevent non-deterministic behavior
Complex CASE WHEN with many branches may be limited by extraction logic (e.g., max 1 case when child)
framework_notes: Implemented as a Cascades RBO rule using thenApply. It modifies join conjuncts directly within the Memo group. It coordinates with OrExpansion and PushDownJoinOtherCondition rules to determine validity. It tracks the condition with the least disjunctions to optimize subsequent OR Expansion steps. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion as it is a logical equivalence transformation.

summary: This rule optimizes a Correlated Apply operator by splitting predicates in the right-child Filter. It pushes uncorrelated predicates down into the right child's input while retaining correlated predicates within the Apply operator's condition, reducing the data volume processed by the correlation logic.
ra_condition: p_corr is not empty (contains references to correlation slots), p_uncorr does not reference correlation slots
correctness_conditions: Uncorrelated predicates must not reference columns from the left input (correlation slots)
The transformation must preserve the Apply semantics (e.g., EXISTS, IN, Outer Apply null handling)
Predicate evaluation must not have side effects that depend on execution order relative to the Apply
performance_impact: Beneficial when uncorrelated predicates are highly selective, significantly reducing the number of rows evaluated against the expensive correlated join condition. Minimal overhead if no uncorrelated predicates exist.
edge_cases: Filters containing only correlated predicates (rule returns original plan)
Filters containing only uncorrelated predicates (rule returns original plan, relies on other rules to remove Apply)
